description: SQL Server monitoring dashboard. Displays buffer cache performance, memory allocation, batch request throughput, lock contention, database sizing, and I/O access patterns for all monitored SQL Server hosts. Metrics sourced from Grafana Alloy via the MSSQL collector.

panels.description: Percentage of data pages found in the buffer cache without requiring a disk read. Values below 95% indicate memory pressure or insufficient buffer pool sizing.
panels.title: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio

panels.description: Average time (in seconds) a data page remains in the buffer pool without being referenced. Values below 300 seconds indicate memory pressure.
panels.title: Page Life Expectancy

panels.description: Total SQL batch requests processed per second across selected hosts. Primary workload throughput indicator.
panels.title: Batch Requests/sec

panels.description: Total number of active user connections across selected SQL Server hosts.
panels.title: User Connections

panels.description: Number of processes currently blocked waiting on locks. Non-zero values indicate lock contention that may degrade application performance.
panels.title: Blocked Processes

panels.description: Rate of deadlock occurrences per second. Any non-zero value warrants investigation into query and transaction design.
panels.title: Deadlocks/sec

panels.description: Batch requests, SQL compilations, and recompilations per second over time. High compilation or recompilation rates relative to batch requests indicate plan cache inefficiency.
panels.title: Batch Requests & Compilations

panels.description: Lock wait rate and deadlock rate over time. Sustained lock waits indicate blocking contention that degrades query throughput.
panels.title: Lock Waits & Deadlocks

panels.description: Total server memory currently allocated vs. target memory determined by the SQL Server memory manager. A persistent gap between total and target indicates memory pressure.
panels.title: Memory: Total vs Target

panels.description: Data file and log file sizes for each database. Large log files relative to data files may indicate infrequent log backups or long-running transactions.
panels.title: Database File Sizes

panels.description: Active transaction count per database over time. Sustained high values may indicate long-running transactions or connection pool exhaustion.
panels.title: Active Transactions

panels.description: Full table scans vs. index seeks per second. A high ratio of full scans to index seeks indicates missing or unused indexes that force table scans.
panels.title: Full Scans vs Index Seeks

panels.description: Page splits per second caused by index page overflow. High rates indicate fragmented indexes that should be rebuilt or reorganized.
panels.title: Page Splits

targets.expr: sum by (hostname, volume) (rate(windows_logical_disk_read_bytes_total{volume!~"HarddiskVolume.*", hostname=~"$hostname", environment=~"$environment", datacenter=~"$datacenter"}[5m]))
sum by (hostname, volume) (rate(windows_logical_disk_write_bytes_total{volume!~"HarddiskVolume.*", hostname=~"$hostname", environment=~"$environment", datacenter=~"$datacenter"}[5m]))

title: SQL Server Overview
